events.label: assistant
events.text:
I wasn't able to run Bash in this session (all commands returned exit code 1 with no output — worth checking your permission settings if you expected shell access), so the diagnosis is based on the scenario description rather than a direct audio waveform/frequency analysis.

Diagnosis written to `/app/answer.json`:

- **Decision: fail** — a closed, energized panel producing an occupant-reported buzz is a classic signature of a loose or degraded termination/splice (contact resistance → localized arcing/heating), not normal equipment hum.
- **Risk: high** — precursor to insulation damage, fire, or arc-flash if left in service.
- Requires immediate lockout/tagout, panel inspection, re-torquing/replacement of the faulty termination per NEC 110.14, and a thermographic check before re-energizing.

If you have a way to expose the actual `.wav` samples to me (e.g., paste a spectrogram image, or fix the shell access), I can refine the frequency/event-time estimates instead of the placeholder values I used.
